It's broken, and we find that at the end, those last three and a half years of the tribulation, by the way, also referred to as the time of Jacob's trouble, again a reference with regard to Israel, that at the end of that seven years, the Lord Jesus Christ, our Messiah, if you read Revelation 19, will return with his saints, not for his saints, but with his saints, and establish his millennial reign, the millennial kingdom of Christ. It is a literal thousand-year reign, and it will be established. You read Revelation 19, and you see all that's taking place. Revelation 20, that Satan will be bound for those thousand years, and all that transpires there, and so we find that the Lord Jesus Christ will establish his millennial reign. It's important, and I emphasize that he returns with his saints, because you have to acknowledge, as you study the New Testament, that the Scriptures plainly say that there's a time in which Jesus is coming for his saints, and there's a time in which he's coming with his saints. And coming for and coming with are two different things, and we see that as the rapture followed by the return. By the way, there's other aspects of that that are important to understand. At the rapture, Jesus comes, and we meet him in the air. At the end of the seven-year tribulation, when he comes back with his saints, his foot actually comes down and is on the Mount of Olives, and all that takes place according to Zechariah chapter 14. And so we find that these things will happen. This 70th week, again, we're in the gap. We don't know exactly, we're not trying to say when it will happen, but we know when the clock starts at the rapture, that seven-year period of time, known as the tribulation. During that period of time, and next week we'll deal with the purpose. Really, there's threefold purpose that we're gonna look at as to why there's going to be this seven-year tribulation. Again, that helps us with answering the when, but understand that in Revelation chapter 6 and verse number 1 is the first seal, and the seven seals go through Revelation 8 and verse number 6. Then you have the seven trumpets, and those seven trumpet judgments start immediately in Revelation 8, verse number 7, and go through Revelation 9 and verse 21. Then there's some things that are recorded for us in the next several chapters of the book of Revelation. Again, things that we will study and we will work through, but you then find the seven vials or the seven bold judgments begin in Revelation 15 verse 1 and go through chapter 16 and verse number 21, and those are, if you will, those three sets of seven major judgments that are going to take place during the the tribulation. Next week, we will look at the purpose of the tribulation. Why is it that God has recorded, why is it that this prophecy is given to Daniel in Daniel chapter number nine, with regard to all of these things, with regard to the timing this 70th week. Again, as we understand the purpose and why God is going to do this, it helps us to answer the question when, but I believe that it's also important with all of that, which I've shared this evening, it's important for us to understand the timeline and to understand what's taking place, because we will begin reading Revelation six in verse number one, and you begin to see that it just immediately starts after we have all that worship that's going on in chapters four and five, and I saw when the lamb opened one of the seals, and bam, that's what we're gonna be seeing with the tribulation. This is the first of those, and by the way, it's a preview for a couple of weeks from now. If you look at some online things, they'll try to tell you that the one that's coming there in Revelation six, one and two is Jesus Christ, and that is a lie. That is the antichrist, and it's important that we interpret scripture properly, and there's so many things out there. No, it's not. It's the antichrist, and so where do we get our doctrine? From the Bible, amen? We wanna be sound in the faith. We wanna have sound doctrine. We get it from the word of God, and we understand that is the antichrist. Again, we'll look at that in a couple of weeks. Next week, the purpose of the tribulation. I know I gave a lot of information this evening, a lot of things with regard to the prophecy. I believe it's important, and it helps us understand as we're going to begin digging into this period of time that Revelation talks about, we're gonna see all of these things that are happening, great destruction, and judgment, and all that's unfolding, and we have to understand why these things are taking place. Why has God given this to us so that we can have an understanding of this 70th week that, as I mentioned already, will be fulfilled literally just as the other 69 weeks have already been fulfilled? Let's pray. Father, we do love you. We thank you so much for loving us. We thank you for your word. We thank you that we can study your word. We thank you that your word is true, that your word is accurate, and we thank you, God, that you have given it to us, even as you mentioned in that portion of scripture to Daniel, to know and to understand, and yes, there are times in which we come to portions of scripture that are more difficult than other portions of scripture, but, God, I pray that you'd help us to be students of the word and that you'd help us not to be tossed to and fro with every wind of doctrine. Lord, help us to be grounded and settled. Lord, I pray that you'd help us to have a clear understanding of these things that we will study through the book of Revelation. I pray, Father, that in the moment of invitation this evening, perhaps some just wanna thank you for your blessings and, Lord, for your word, however it is that you may have worked in hearts and lives, would you help us tonight to be responsive? Well, thank you, God, for what you'll do for it's in Christ's name we pray. Would you stand?
